label: Capitol Hill
subject: How a U.S. bill becomes a law
bossHook: Take a bill from a rough draft, through both chambers of Congress, to the President's signature — in an order that actually works.

bill-order.title: First Things First
bill-order.hook: A bill can't be voted on before it exists. Put these three in an order that works.
bill-order.winText: Draft → introduce → vote. Order matters: each step depends on the one before it.

prerequisites.title: The Foundation
prerequisites.hook: Some steps unlock others. Find the one thing that must happen first, then build on it.
prerequisites.winText: The draft came first — you can't introduce or co-sponsor a bill that doesn't exist. That's a prerequisite.

failText.wrong-start: You started with a step that needs something else first. Begin with the draft — it needs nothing.
failText.dependency-violation: That step happened before its prerequisite. A bill can't move before it's drafted.

the-chain.title: Through Both Chambers
the-chain.hook: Introduce it, pass it in BOTH the House and the Senate, then send it to the President. Trace the chain.
the-chain.winText: The House and the Senate both passed it before it reached the President's desk. You traced the whole chain.

failText.wrong-start: Start with the step that needs nothing — draft the bill.
failText.dependency-violation: You passed or signed it before an earlier step existed. Trace what each step needs.

becomes-law.title: BOSS · Becomes a Law
becomes-law.hook: Six steps, two chambers that must converge. Take a bill from a rough draft all the way to law.
becomes-law.winText: The bill cleared both chambers in parallel, then converged on the President's signature. That's how a bill becomes a law.

failText.wrong-start: Begin with the step that needs nothing — draft the bill.
failText.dependency-violation: A step ran before something it depends on. Both chambers must pass it before the President can sign.

missing-foundation.title: SIDE-QUEST · Draft It First
missing-foundation.hook: You keep building before the bill exists. Drill it: place the ONE step everything else needs, first.
missing-foundation.winText: Draft first — everything else depends on it. The foundation is whatever needs nothing and unlocks the rest.

failText.wrong-start: Still starting in the wrong place — lead with the draft, which needs nothing.
failText.dependency-violation: Put the draft before the steps that depend on it.

heal-order-matters.title: SIDE-QUEST · One Step at a Time
heal-order-matters.hook: Three steps, one straight line. A bill has to exist before it moves, and move before it's voted on. Put them in the only order that works.
heal-order-matters.winText: Draft → introduce → vote. Each step needs the one right before it, so there's exactly one order that runs. That's what 'order matters' means.

failText.wrong-start: You began with a step that needs something first. Lead with the draft — it's the only step that needs nothing.
failText.dependency-violation: A step ran before the one it depends on. A bill can't be introduced before it's drafted, or voted on before it's introduced.
